name: natural-developer-task-suite
purpose: Focused natural-prompt suite for proving TokenOpt + CodeGraph can support common developer workflows without forcing tool-specific prompt scripts.

tasks.prompt: Return valid compact JSON only with keys summary, task_classes, natural_routing, evidence_sources, risks. Research what developer tasks this repo is designed to support, including research, investigate code, investigate PBI, implement, unit-test, review, and Spec Kit workflows. Explain when a natural prompt should use a context broker versus direct bounded code evidence.
qualityRubric: explains natural tool routing without naming mandatory tool scripts
gateAssertions: no broad fallback after answerable evidence

tasks.prompt: Return valid compact JSON only with keys pbi_summary, given_when_then, how_it_should_work, why_it_matters, impacted_files, tests_or_validation, open_questions. Investigate this PBI: Given a developer asks naturally for TokenOpt + CodeGraph benchmark evidence, When the task is broad or unknown-owner, Then the agent should pick compact context first and only bounded followups; When the task names a file/symbol/diff, Then it should go exact first. Explain how and why the repo supports that behavior.
qualityRubric: explains why the behavior reduces token burn
gateAssertions: PBI evidence requires business and source grounding

tasks.prompt: Return valid compact JSON only with keys scope, files_to_change, implementation_steps, business_coverage, tests_to_add, validation_commands, compatibility_risks. Create an implementation handoff for improving natural TokenOpt + CodeGraph benchmarking so it preserves user/agent instructions, keeps prompts natural, and proves business behavior coverage with unit/regression tests, not just method coverage.
gateAssertions: unit tests cover behavior not only method names

tasks.prompt: Return valid compact JSON only with keys spec_intent, tokenopt_detection, phases, evidence_reuse, validation, risks. Basic Spec Kit prompt: `/speckit.specify Add a natural TokenOpt + CodeGraph benchmark mode that reduces token burn while preserving output quality.` Explain how the repo should detect whether TokenOpt evidence is useful during specify/plan/tasks/implement, without forcing the user to name TokenOpt or CodeGraph tools.
gateAssertions: Spec Kit prompt can remain basic
TokenOpt should be adaptive not mandatory

tasks.prompt: Return valid compact JSON only with keys target_behavior, owner_source_files, existing_test_files, tests_to_add, assertions, validation_commands, risks. Write a unit/regression test plan for the behavior: natural benchmark prompts should strip output-format boilerplate from retrieval intent but keep the original user prompt as the final-answer contract.
qualityRubric: targets behavior not just method coverage
gateAssertions: write_unittest route requires target behavior and tests
no broad fallback after enough evidence
